questions.question: Define an array of structures and explain its need with a real-world example.
keyPoints: Array of structures is a collection of structure variables stored consecutively in memory
Needed when multiple records of heterogeneous data must be stored and managed together
Real-world example: Student database storing roll number, name, marks, and attendance for 1000 students
Allows organizing related data of different types under one unit

questions.question: How is memory allocated for an array of structures? Calculate the total memory required for an array of 50 structures where each structure contains: int a (4 bytes), char b (1 byte), float c (4 bytes).
keyPoints: Memory is allocated contiguously equal to sizeof(struct) × number of elements
Structure may have padding for alignment
Size: 4 + 1 + 3(padding) + 4 = 12 bytes per structure
Total memory: 12 × 50 = 600 bytes
Each element occupies 12 bytes at consecutive memory locations

questions.question: Write a C program to declare an array of 5 structures representing complex numbers (real and imaginary parts), initialize them, and display all complex numbers.
keyPoints: Define struct Complex with float real and imag members
Declare array: struct Complex nums[5]
Initialize using designated initializers or loop
Display using printf with formatting
Show access via array index and dot operator

questions.question: Explain the different methods to initialize an array of structures in C with examples.
keyPoints: Method 1: Initialization at declaration - struct Student s[2] = {{101, "John"}, {102, "Alice"}}
Method 2: Partial initialization - struct Student s[3] = {{101}} (remaining zero-initialized)
Method 3: C99 designated initializers - struct Student s[2] = {[0].roll=101, [1].roll=102}
Method 4: Using loops and scanf for runtime input
Each element must be enclosed in its own braces when initializing

questions.question: How do you pass an array of structures to a function? Explain with an example showing how modifications inside the function affect the original array.
keyPoints: Array name decays to pointer to first element
Function declaration: void func(struct Student s[], int n) or void func(struct Student *s, int n)
Both pass address, allowing modification of original
Inside function, access via s[i].member or (ptr+i)->member
Changes made inside function affect the original array

questions.question: Write a C program to search for a student by roll number in an array of 10 student structures and display their details if found.
keyPoints: Define struct Student with roll, name, marks
Store 10 student records in array
Accept roll number to search from user
Use linear search: iterate and compare roll numbers
Display student details on match, 'Not Found' message otherwise
Handle case when multiple matches exist if needed

questions.question: What are the advantages and limitations of using array of structures?
keyPoints: Advantages: Organized heterogeneous data, easy access, efficient memory, supports sorting/searching, represents real-world entities
Limitations: Fixed size (must know maximum elements), contiguous memory requirement, insertion/deletion inefficient, potential memory wastage if underutilized

questions.question: Write a complete C program to manage a library system using array of structures. The program should: (a) Store information of 100 books (accession number, title, author, price), (b) Display all books, (c) Search by author, (d) Search by title, (e) List books by price range.
keyPoints: Define struct Book with all required fields
Create array struct Book library[100]
Implement menu-driven interface with switch-case
Display function: iterate and print all books
Author search: linear search comparing author field
Title search: linear search or use strstr() for substring
Price range: compare price >= min && price <= max
Use appropriate loops and string functions
Handle case sensitivity and edge cases

questions.question: Explain the concept of pointer to structure and its relationship with array of structures. Write a C program to traverse an array of structures using both array index notation and pointer notation.
keyPoints: Pointer to structure stores address of a structure variable
When pointing to array element, pointer arithmetic works: ptr+i points to element i
Access members: (*ptr).member or ptr->member
Can iterate array using ptr++ to move between elements
Show equivalence: arr[i].x same as *(arr+i).x same as (arr+i)->x
Program demonstrates both iteration methods
Explain memory layout and contiguous storage

questions.question: Write a C program to sort an array of 50 employee structures based on salary (ascending order) using bubble sort. Each employee has: emp_id, name, department, salary. After sorting, display the sorted list.
keyPoints: Define struct Employee with emp_id, name, department, salary
Create array of 50 elements, populate with data
Implement bubble sort: compare salaries of adjacent elements
Use temporary variable for swapping entire structures
After sort, iterate and display in sorted order
Show before and after comparison for verification
Use functions for display and sort for modularity

questions.question: A student record contains: roll number, name, marks in 5 subjects, total, and percentage. Write a C program to (a) accept data for n students, (b) calculate total and percentage, (c) find topper, (d) display all records in tabular format, (e) search by roll number.
keyPoints: Define struct Student with all required fields including marks[5]
Accept n from user, allocate/declare array of n structures
For each student: input marks, calculate sum and percentage
Find topper: track max percentage while inputting or in separate pass
Display formatted table with proper alignment and headers
Search: linear search by roll number, display record if found
Handle invalid roll number gracefully
Use functions for modular code: input, calculate, display, search
